title: fix(docs): Fix glossary FIXME tags
body:
#### Reference Issues/PRs
Fixes #31628 

#### What does this implement/fix? Explain your changes.


#### Any other comments?
While working on #31628, I encountered a `ModuleNotFoundError` due to a misnamed Sphinx extension (`sphinx-prompt` instead of `sphinx_prompt`). This PR also fixes that to ensure local doc builds (`make`) work correctly.

#### Setup
Python 3.13.3 (macOS 15.5 arm64)
... (dependencies listed)

step_plan.summary: Fix the documentation build by correcting the Sphinx extension module name in `doc/conf.py`, and clean up `FIXME` markers in `doc/glossary.rst` by replacing them with accurate, current glossary wording.

steps.operation: Fix Sphinx extension module name in the extensions list (and/or any explicit extension loading) so the docs build can import it successfully
steps.reason: Sphinx extensions must be specified by their importable Python module name; using the hyphenated form ("sphinx-prompt") can trigger ModuleNotFoundError during `make html`. Ensuring the correct module name ("sphinx_prompt") restores local documentation builds and aligns with Python import rules.
steps.side_effects: Doc builds will start importing the correct extension; if environments previously relied on the incorrect name (unlikely), they’ll now require the actual `sphinx-prompt` package installed. CI docs build may change behavior if it previously skipped/failed early; verify `make html` and any doc build jobs still pass.

steps.operation: Remove/replace glossary `FIXME`-tagged wording (e.g., in the `meta-estimator` entry) with accurate, up-to-date phrasing about current scikit-learn behavior
steps.reason: FIXME markers in published documentation are stale TODOs and degrade doc quality; issue #31628 tracks cleaning these up. The glossary text should reflect current Pipeline/FeatureUnion (or other referenced components) behavior without TODO annotations, using correct terminology and constraints.
steps.side_effects: Rendered glossary content will change; may require minor reflow/wrapping adjustments in the .rst. If other docs reference the old wording verbatim, they may need updates (search for exact phrases). Doc linting (RST formatting) could fail if spacing/indentation is off—rebuild docs to confirm.
